title: Quality Assurance Specialist (Product Distribution)
description:
**Site Name:** Italy \- Verona  

**Posted Date:** Apr 22 2026 *We create a place where people can grow, be their best, be safe, and feel welcome, valued, and included. We offer a competitive salary, an annual bonus based on company performance, healthcare and wellbeing programmes, pension plan membership, and shares and savings programme.*

*We embrace modern work practises; our Performance with Choice programme offers a hybrid working model, empowering you to find the optimal balance between remote and in\-office work.*

*Discover more about our company wide benefits and life at GSK on our webpage* *Life at GSK \| GSK*

**Scope:**


The job exists within Italy Commercial Organization (Local Operating Companies\-LOC) to ensure that the mandated GSK Quality Management Systems – QMS \- requirements are met.


Provides effective and efficient quality assurance systems support to the Italian Quality Manager, including operational activities related the distribution of GSK and ViiV medicines, vaccines and Medical Devices according to GDP guidance including but not limited to any local administrative release, complaint handling, management of product returns and repacking activities.


The function is critical to the effective management of processes, systems and activities that are crucial or have a direct impact on product quality and regulatory compliance. The job holder is expected to apply and sustain the QMS in the LOC and at third parties warehouses, including encouraging the Commercial Business to own quality activities in their day to day work.

**In this role you will:**

**Quality Strategy**

* Heighten and promote quality and QMS awareness in the commercial business functions.
* Engage and assist relevant stakeholders in understanding and generating appropriate systems, processes and policies, as required within defined areas of the business, in compliance with QMS principles.
* Maintain a high level of QMS knowledge and awareness of changes within the commercial environment to seek opportunities and manage potential business adversity.
* Develop a national, regional and Eu network to obtain regulatory intelligence and provide the necessary analysis to understand and escalate issues which have the potential to impact patients, products, supplies or GSK’s reputation.

**Key Activities**

* Manage operational activities (day to day work) and interaction with internal and external stakeholders to ensure compliance with products and their distribution on the market (Good Distribution Practice).
* Work with 3rd party service providers to assure alignment in stocks management.
* Manage local Deviations, CAPA, Change Control, Complaints and any other products incidents, assess impact and ensure corrective and preventative actions and CAPA effectiveness.
* Supervise the management of products returned from the market.
* Define Distribution Risk Assessments (DRA) for product distributed from the Italian MMW and review DRA for products received from manufacturing sites.
* Perform Management Monitoring in compliance with GSK QMS requirements.
* Ensure local operations are in full compliance with GSK standards and policies (QMS), GxP and regulatory requirements.
* Contribute to deliver a quality culture throughout all LOC functions.

***Why You?***

**Basic Qualifications**

**We are looking for professionals with these required skills to achieve our goals:**

* Master’s degree in Pharmacy, Chemistry, Biology, Life Sciences or similar (scientific disciplines)
* Fluent Italian and English, both written and spoken (English at least B2 level)
* General computer literacy with Intermediate skills in Microsoft Word, Excel, Power Point and Outlook.

**Preferred Qualifications**

**Please note the following skills are not necessary, just preferred, if you do not have them, please still apply:**

* Previous experience (even short) within the Pharmaceutical industries, preferably in the capacity of a quality specialist
* Experience in quality assurance systems, particularly in the areas of batch release, product incidents, artwork control and performing audits.
* Knowledge of regulatory requirements pertaining to GMP/GDP (GxP) and good knowledge on effective quality documentation system.
* Excellent written and verbal communication, fostering positive relationships with stakeholders at all levels, and working effectively in cross\-functional teams.
* Detail\-oriented with strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, excellent time management, and the ability to uphold standards while effectively and persuasively addressing QA situations.
* Open to innovation, flexible in thinking, and able to consider diverse perspectives for continuous improvement.

***What We Offer:***

* Permanent contract in a very Inclusive environment
* Smart Working up to 50%
* Performance Reward
* Flexible Benefits
* Company Healthcare Plan
* Integrative pension fund
* Employee Assistance Programme
* Tax assistance
* Personal parcel delivery service
* Local nursery agreement
* Free Company canteen
* Coffee Corners

**Closing Date for Applications – 8 May 2026**

*Please take a copy of the Job Description, as this will not be available post closure of the advert.*  

*When applying for this role, please use the ‘cover letter’ of the online application or your CV to describe how you meet the competencies for this role, as outlined in the job requirements above. The information that you have provided in your cover letter and CV will be used to assess your application.*

**Why GSK?**

**Uniting science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together.**


GSK is a global biopharma company with a purpose to unite science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together. We aim to positively impact the health of 2\.5 billion people by the end of the decade, as a successful, growing company where people can thrive. We get ahead of disease by preventing and treating it with innovation in specialty medicines and vaccines. We focus on four therapeutic areas: respiratory, immunology and inflammation; oncology; HIV; and infectious diseases – to impact health at scale.


People and patients around the world count on the medicines and vaccines we make, so we’re committed to creating an environment where our people can thrive and focus on what matters most. Our culture of being ambitious for patients, accountable for impact and doing the right thing is the foundation for how, together, we deliver for patients, shareholders and our people.


Should you require any specific adjustments to our process to assist you in demonstrating your strengths and capabilities contact us on ita\-inclusion.recruitment@gsk.com.


Please note should your enquiry not relate to adjustments, we will not be able to support you through these channels.


GSK is an Equal Opportunity Employer. This ensures that all qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), parental status, national origin, age, disability, genetic information (including family medical history), military service or any basis prohibited under federal, state or local law.

**Important notice to Employment businesses/ Agencies**


GSK does not accept referrals from employment businesses and/or employment agencies in respect of the vacancies posted on this site. All employment businesses/agencies are required to contact GSK's commercial and general procurement/human resources department to obtain prior written authorization before referring any candidates to GSK. The obtaining of prior written authorization is a condition precedent to any agreement (verbal or written) between the employment business/ agency and GSK. In the absence of such written authorization being obtained any actions undertaken by the employment business/agency shall be deemed to have been performed without the consent or contractual agreement of GSK. GSK shall therefore not be liable for any fees arising from such actions or any fees arising from any referrals by employment businesses/agencies in respect of the vacancies posted on this site.


Please note that if you are a US Licensed Healthcare Professional or Healthcare Professional as defined by the laws of the state issuing your license, GSK may be required to capture and report expenses GSK incurs, on your behalf, in the event you are afforded an interview for employment. This capture of applicable transfers of value is necessary to ensure GSK’s compliance to all federal and state US Transparency requirements. For more information, please visit the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) website at https://openpaymentsdata.cms.gov/

title: Process Engineer
description:
**Requistion ID**: 83084

  

Dentsply Sirona is the world’s largest manufacturer of professional dental products and technologies, with over a century of innovation and service to the dental industry and patients worldwide. Dentsply Sirona develops, manufactures, and markets a comprehensive solutions offering including dental and oral health products as well as other consumable medical devices under a strong portfolio of world class brands.

  

Dentsply Sirona’s products provide innovative, high\-quality, and effective solutions to advance patient care and deliver better and safer dental care. Dentsply Sirona’s headquarter is located in Charlotte, North Carolina. The company’s shares are listed in the United States on NASDAQ under the symbol XRAY. Visit www.dentsplysirona.com for more information about Dentsply Sirona and its products.

  

For our manufacturing site in **Badia Polesine**, we are looking for a:

 **Process Engineer**

  

The role will be responsible for the development, optimization, and validation of production processes, supporting manufacturing in troubleshooting activities and driving continuous improvement initiatives. The Process Engineer will also prepare process documentation (work instructions) and train new operators on the correct use of production lines.

 **Responsibilities**

* In support of the maintenance team, ensures the correct operation and efficiency of equipment in compliance with applicable Safety, Environmental, and Quality procedures
* Responsible for the validation of production processes (IQ/OQ/PQ), definition of process parameters, and drafting of operating instructions in accordance with industry standards (e.g. ISO 13485 for medical devices)
* Monitors process stability and manages deviations
* Delivers technical training to new operators on the management of 3D printing lines, post‑processing, and quality control, periodically assessing acquired skills and providing continuous on‑the‑job support
* Manages the commissioning and testing of equipment, organizing Factory Acceptance Tests (FAT) and Site Acceptance Tests (SAT), and overseeing start‑up, calibration, and optimization of control logic
* Provides technical support to Production and Engineering for process analysis and problem solving (troubleshooting)
* Defines process KPIs and performs data analysis to improve efficiency, quality, and reduce scrap
* Implements Lean/Kaizen methodologies to improve productivity and ergonomics
* Participates in cross‑functional teams for new projects related to the introduction of new 3D printing and post‑processing technologies
* Coordinates external suppliers and system integrators during on‑site activities and ensures that all required technical documentation (drawings, manuals, declarations of conformity) is properly prepared and filed
* Supports the QAS Manager (Quality, Environment \& Safety Manager) in managing relationships with regulatory and certification bodies

 **Skills \& Qualifications**

* **Education:** Degree in Materials Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, or equivalent
* **Experience:** At least 3 years in a similar role
* Knowledge of resin‑based 3D printing processes (SLA, DLP, or similar)
* Data analysis skills (Excel, Minitab, or equivalent software)
* Knowledge of Lean Manufacturing techniques (5S, SMED, VSM) and Statistical Process Control (SPC)
* Experience with Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA)
* Technical drawing skills (reading P\&ID diagrams, electrical schematics, automation layouts)
* Knowledge of medical device regulations (ISO 13485, cGMP) – preferred
* Fluent Italian and good command of technical English
* Flexibility: ability to adapt to different contexts and collaborate effectively with colleagues and managers
* Active participation in continuous improvement initiatives
* Strong problem‑solving mindset
* High level of accuracy, analytical approach, and strong quality orientation

 **Equal Opportunity Employer:** Dentsply Sirona is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will be considered without unlawful discrimination or regard for race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, sexual or gender identity, national or ethnic origin, age, marital status, disability, genetic factors, military and veteran status, or any other characteristics protected by applicable local law.

**Eligibility:** All successful applicants must be eligible to work in the country the position is based.

**Assistance:** If you need assistance with completing the online application due to a disability, please send an accommodation request to accommodationrequest@dentsplysirona.com.

**Agencies:** Please note that Dentsply Sirona does not accept or respond to unsolicited requests or applications submitted by Recruitment Agencies/ Search Firms.

**Notice on Fraudulent Job Offers:** Unfortunately, we are aware of third parties that pretend to represent our company offering unauthorized employment opportunities. If you think a fraudulent source is offering you a job, please have a look at the following information: careers.dentsplysirona.com.
company_description: Dentsply Sirona is the world’s largest manufacturer of professional dental products and technologies.

title: Program and National Test Center Coordinator, Vicenza
description:
Job Ref:
10025321
Location:
Vicenza\-Italy
Category:
Advising and Enrollment
Type:
Full time

**Program and National Test Center Coordinator**

**UMGC Europe**

**Overseas Contingent III, Full\-Time 100% FTE, Grade 003**

**Location: Vicenza, Italy**


The Program and National Test Center Coordinator serves as the on\-site University of Maryland Global Campus representative responsible for providing academic, administrative and testing support services, community outreach, and customer relations management. The Program and National Test Center Coordinator is a front\-line staff member that provides the best possible services to current and prospective students and engages as a team member. This position reports directly to the Site Lead (SL), and if no Site Lead is assigned to the site, to the Assistant Director (AD). The Program and National Test Center Coordinator must work effectively with the AD and the Regional Director (RD), local Program Coordinators and staff, education center personnel, faculty, administrative staff , and the military community at large. There will at times be site specific duties that differ from location to location. Occasional evening/weekend hours; travel may be required.

**SPECIFIC RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE:**

* Provide students with superior customer service as an advocate and advisor throughout the academic cycle in a fast\-paced environment, using various communication channels, to include in\-person visits, telephone, e\-mail, and videoconferencing.
* Promote and support localized UMGC services to include hybrid classes, testing services, and university sponsored events as assigned at site(s).
* Provide operational and administrative duties as needed to ensure offices and test centers (if applicable) are properly maintained by test agency standards.
* Maintain and expand knowledge and proficiency with UMGC systems to include all customer relationship management (CRM) databases to include Salesforce, Peoplesoft, and other university sponsored systems.
* Document all student contact through UMGC's Customer Relations Management System and schedule appropriate follow\-up activities
* Provide daily customer service and communicate with prospective and current students in a fast\-paced, in person environment and also, through phone, email, CRM, and video communications
* Maintain a positive relationship with the Education Services Officer (ESO), Specialists/Counselors, military partners, and equivalents. Provide periodic briefings as allowed to update military representatives on UMGC programs of interest.
* Organize and participate in local outreach activities to include, Education Fairs, Open Houses, Semester Start\-ups, Student Appreciation Days, Local Graduations and other events as needed, professionally representing UMGC
* Track and analyze class enrollments and student interest, as well as local degree progression, to provide input on scheduling; ensure classrooms are provided with adequate space and needed technology for class instruction; be present at the first class session to provide assistance to instructors and students; and assist faculty in obtaining base access to attend class on their assigned installation
* Provide assistance with processing students' military documentation (i.e. Tuition Assistance forms, orders, etc.)
* Distribute marketing, advertising, and mass media materials at assigned locations when permissible
* Execute site specific outreach plans and participate in outreach events to support university goals towards recruitment and retention
* Provide National Test Center testing services on a basis that meets community demand while balancing other, outlined duties and maintain NTC trainings/certifications as required
* Participate in UMGC staff meetings, professional development activities and training sessions as required
* Track and compile monthly statistics and provide reports as needed
* Maintain appropriate communication with immediate supervisor and other UMGC staff to ensure timely resolution of student issues/escalations
* Other job\-related duties as assigned

**REQUIRED EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:**

* Bachelor's Degree from a U.S. regionally accredited institution or foreign degree equivalent
* Background in customer service
* Able to work in a fast\-paced and complex environment with accurate attention to detail
* Possess basic office management and clerical skills
* Effective written and verbal communication skills
* Good interpersonal skills
* Successful applicants must be able to pass background check and meet requirements for individual logistic support (ILS).

**PREFERRED EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:**

* Applicants with a U.S. valid driver's license and ability to obtain military driver's permit in the host nation.

**WHO MAY APPLY:** Local applicants with individual logistical support (ILS) only. Applicants must already have and be able to maintain ILS, meaning that they must possess a U.S. military ID card and SOFA card from an external source, as this position does not offer ILS. For employment eligibility purposes, applicant must qualify for ILS as described in USAREUR Regulation 600\-700, Section III, 7\-13\. Criteria under which applicants qualify for ILS are: must serve the US Forces exclusively; are not stateless persons; are nationals of a NATO state, excluding the host nation; are not ordinarily resident in the host nation. Must have a valid passport.

**All submissions should include a cover letter and resume**.

**The University of Maryland Global Campus (UMGC) is an equal opportunity employer and complies with all applicable federal and state laws regarding nondiscrimination. UMGC is committed to a policy of equal opportunity for all persons and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, age, marital status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, disability, religion, ancestry, political affiliation or veteran status in employment, educational programs and activities, and admissions.**

**Workplace Accommodations:**


The University of Maryland Global Campus Global Campus (UMGC) is committed to creating and maintaining a welcoming and inclusive working environment for people of all abilities. UMGC is dedicated to the principle that no qualified individual with a disability shall, based on disability, be excluded from participation in or be denied the benefits of the services, programs, or activities of the University, or be subjected to discrimination. For information about UMGC's Reasonable Workplace Accommodation Policy or to request an accommodation, applicants/candidates can contact Employee Accommodations via email at employee\-accommodations@umgc.edu.

**Benefits Package Highlights:**

* **Generous Time Off:** Enjoy between 15 and 25 days of paid vacation time off, depending on your years of service, 15 days of sick leave, 3 personal days, and 11 Federal holidays. **Part\-Time rates will be prorated based on Full\-Time Equivalency.**
* **Comprehensive Health Coverage:** Access to health care, medical with vision, dental, and prescription plans for both individuals and families, effective from the 1st of the month following your hire date. Contingent III employees who work on average 20 or more hours per week (130 hours per month) are eligible for subsidized state of Maryland medical and prescription benefits.
* **Insurance Options:** Term Life Insurance and Accidental Death and Dismemberment Insurance, and Long\-Term Disability (LTD) Insurance. **NOTE: CIII Part\-time employees working less than 0\.5 FTE are not eligible for LTD.**
* **Retirement Stipend:** Contingent III employees receive a retirement stipend equal to 7\.25 percent of their base salary and are eligible to contribute to a supplemental retirement account (SRA).
* **Supplemental Retirement Plans**: include 403(b), 457(b), and various Roth options. The university does not provide subsidies or matching funds.
* **Tuition Remission:** CIII employees are eligible immediately for Tuition Remission at UMGC or at University of Maryland College Park. Spouses and dependent children are eligible for undergraduate tuition remission after two years of service**. NOTE: For part\-time employees (at least 50 percent of the time), tuition remission benefits are prorated.**
* **Moving Allowance:** May be available based on terms, conditions, and agreements of the position.


For additional information see:UMGC Benefits Overview for Overseas Contingent III Employees

**Hiring Rate:**


$38,200\.00**Additional Compensation:**


Employees receive the applicable currency adjustment (CAX) subject to monthly DoD COLA Index for the work location, which is subject to change and fluctuation based on market indices.
company_description: University of Maryland Global Campus (UMGC, formerly UMUC) is the largest public university in the United States.

title: Marketing & Communication Specialist
description:
**Magnolia S.r.l.**, società accreditata dalla Regione Veneto specializzata in **finanziamenti alle imprese** e **formazione aziendale**, ricerca un/una **Marketing \& Communication Specialist** per il proprio ufficio marketing.

**Chi siamo**

Siamo partner d'impresa per le PMI venete: aiutiamo le aziende a ottenere contributi a fondo perduto e a crescere attraverso formazione qualificata. Certificati ISO 9001 e qualificati Fondimpresa, dal 2009 supportiamo lo sviluppo delle imprese con un team di professionisti dedicati.

**La posizione**

La risorsa avrà la responsabilità di coordinare e sovrintendere alla **comunicazione integrata** delle attività di Magnolia, con particolare focus su:

* **Servizi alle imprese**: bandi regionali e nazionali, contributi a fondo perduto, consulenza, Fondimpresa
* **Offerta formativa**: corsi a catalogo e corsi rivolti alle persone

**Responsabilità principali**

· Pianificare e gestire la strategia di comunicazione multi\-canale (web, social media, newsletter, materiali offline)

* Occuparsi della promozione online e offline di bandi, opportunità e corsi formativi
* Gestire il sito web e i canali social aziendali
* Sviluppare campagne di lead generation e customer engagement
* Monitorare performance e KPI delle attività di marketing
* Collaborare con il team di Magnolia offrendo supporto marketing alle attività
* Curare la corporate identity e il posizionamento del brand

**Requisiti richiesti**

· Laurea (preferibilmente in Marketing, Comunicazione, Economia o discipline affini)

* Esperienza di **almeno 1 anno** in ruoli di marketing e comunicazione
* Buona conoscenza degli strumenti di digital marketing (SEO, SEM, social media, email marketing)
* Capacità di copywriting e content creation
* Conoscenza dei principali strumenti di grafica e CMS (es. WordPress, Canva, Adobe Suite)
* Competenze analitiche (Google Analytics, Meta Business Suite)

**Competenze preferenziali:**

* Esperienza nel settore B2B o servizi alle imprese
* Conoscenza del mondo della formazione aziendale e dei bandi pubblici
* Familiarità con marketing automation e CRM

**Caratteristiche personali**

* Autonomia operativa e capacità organizzative
* Creatività e proattività
* Orientamento ai risultati
* Ottime capacità relazionali e di lavoro in team
* Passione per il mondo digital

**Cosa offriamo**

* Contratto a tempo determinato/indeterminato (da valutare in base al profilo)
* Inserimento in un team dinamico e qualificato
* Ambiente stimolante e positivo con focus su innovazione e crescita
* Opportunità di formazione continua

**Come candidarsi**

Gli interessati possono inviare CV con autorizzazione al trattamento dei dati personali (D.Lgs. 196/2003 e GDPR 679/2016\) a:

**gestione@magnoliapartner.it**

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo pieno

Retribuzione: €1\.400,00 \- €1\.600,00 al mese

Sede di lavoro: Di persona

title: Consulente tecnico e formatore per la salute e sicurezza sul lavoro
description:
Siamo in forte crescita. E cerchiamo la persona giusta, non quella più disponibile.

In Overall Group lavoriamo ogni giorno per rendere la sicurezza sul lavoro qualcosa di concreto, comprensibile e utile per le aziende.

Non siamo in cerca di numeri o perdi\-tempo.

Non siamo in cerca di apprendisti.

Siamo in cerca di qualità, immediata e ben pagata.

Chi cerchiamo:

Un/a **Consulente Tecnico Salute e Sicurezza** che sappia gestire la complessità normativa in autonomia.

Con laurea tecnica (Ingegneria, Tecnico della Prevenzione o similare) e almeno 2 anni di esperienza nel settore.

Quello che fa davvero la differenza per noi:

* Sai comunicare con equilibrio anche nelle situazioni difficili
* Vuoi crescere, non solo lavorare
* Quando c'è un problema, analizzi e risolvi, non aspetti, proponi soluzioni
* Sei affidabile e hai cura delle persone e del cliente

Offriamo un percorso reale: autonomia, formazione continua, crescita professionale ed economica meritocratica.

Auto, cellulare e strumenti aziendali inclusi.

Poche promesse, solo risultati concreti misurati sul valore delle persone.

**Cosa farai in Overall Group**

* Sopralluoghi aziendali
* Redazione documenti (DVR, POS, DUVRI, procedure operative)
* Incarichi esterni di RSPP (con copertura assicurativa/legale massima)
* Assistenza tecnica/consulenza in ambito salute e sicurezza
* Attività di Formazione in aula e presso aziende clienti

**Requisiti preferenziali**

* Titolo di studio tecnico (ingegneria, prevenzione, sicurezza, etc...)
* RSPP/ASPP
* Formatore qualificato
* Esperienza nel settore sicurezza (2 anni)
* Patente B e disponibilità a spostamenti in zona Verona e limitrofi
* Ottima capacità comunicativa
* Capacità di utilizzo strumenti digitali

**Cosa offriamo**

* Inserimento in un team giovane, collaborativo e orientatato al fare
* Formazione continua tecnica
* Percorso di crescita chiaro e supportato
* Autonomia operativa e responsabilità graduale
* Ambiente di lavoro sereno, competente e meritocratico
* Smartworking (post percorso di inserimento fisso)

**Abilitazione/Certificazione:**

* Patente B (Obbligatorio)
* Laurea Ingegneria/Tecnico Prevenzione/Sicurezza sul lavoro (Obbligatorio)

Se ti riconosci, hai voglia di emergere e fartelo riconoscere, mandami un messaggio diretto o invia la tua candidatura.

Tazio Pradella

Overall Group Srl — Consulenza Salute e Sicurezza sul Lavoro

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo indeterminato

Retribuzione: €23\.000,00 \- €26\.000,00 all'anno

Benefit:

* Auto aziendale
* Bevande gratis
* Cellulare aziendale
* Computer aziendale
* Lavoro da casa
* Mensa aziendale
* Parcheggio libero
* Supporto allo sviluppo professionale

Domande di preselezione:

* Esperienza come tecnico/formatore e consulente e in ambito Salute e Sicurezza

Sede di lavoro: Di persona

title: Operatore telemarketing
description:
L'Agenzia Etika Immobiliare cerca una figura di Telemarketing

Chi siamo:

Etika Immobiliare è un’agenzia dinamica, giovanile e in forte crescita, con una visione chiara: portare innovazione, trasparenza e risultati concreti nell' immobiliare. Crediamo nel valore delle persone e nell’importanza di un approccio moderno al mercato immobiliare.

Cosa cerchiamo:

Stiamo cercando una figura con ottime capacità comunicative, determinata e orientata al raggiungimento degli obbiettivi. Il tuo ruolo sarà supportare l’agenzia nell’acquisizione di nuovi contatti qualificati, sviluppando relazioni con potenziali clienti e mantenendo aggiornato il nostro database. Non è necessaria esperienza pregressa nel settore: cerchiamo sopratutto entusiasmo e voglia di imparare.

Cosa farai:

Contatto telefonico con potenziali clienti (venditori e acquirenti).

Ricerca e qualificazione di nuovi lead attraverso database e strumenti digitali.

Gestione e aggiornamento del CRM aziendale.

Requisiti:

Ottime capacità comunicative e relazionali

Attitudine al contatto telefonico e al lavoro in team

Determinazione e voglia di crescere professionalmente

Offriamo:

Contratto Part\-time

Orari: Dalle 15\.00 alle 19\.00

Compenso fisso mensile 600€ \+ piano provvigionale legato ai risultati

Formazione continua e possibilità di crescita nel settore immobiliare

Contratto di lavoro: Part\-time

Retribuzione: €600,00 al mese

Benefit:

* Orario flessibile
* Supporto allo sviluppo professionale

Sede di lavoro: Di persona

title: IT Business Analyst – Regulatory Compliance (DORA/NIS2)
description:
**Proxima** è un gruppo di aziende con skill funzionali e tecnologiche innovative, che mettono a fattor comune la propria esperienza in un’offerta congiunta di sviluppo di soluzioni all’interno della nostra Software Factory. Offriamo consulenza e servizi di Application Management e Quality Assurance.

**Chi cerchiamo?**

Siamo alla ricerca di un\* **IT Business Analyst / PMO – Compliance (DORA, NIS2\)** per un importante progetto in ambito **compliance normativa ICT**.

**Dove e come?**

Prevista **presenza on site 5 giorni su 5 a Verona (zona Stazione Porta Nuova)**

**Di cosa ti occuperai?**

Entrerai a far parte del team di progetto e ti occuperai di:

* Raccolta, analisi e organizzazione della documentazione richiesta in ambito **DORA** e **NIS2**
* Supporto alla stesura, revisione e aggiornamento di **procedure operative** e documentazione di compliance
* Coordinamento operativo tra il cliente e la capogruppo per garantire l’avanzamento delle iniziative
* Supporto alle attività di **allineamento normativo e operativo** in ambito ICT
* Monitoraggio delle attività, follow\-up e gestione delle priorità progettuali
* Supporto alla governance progettuale e alle attività di PMO
* Interazione con stakeholder business e IT per la raccolta delle informazioni necessarie

**Hai queste competenze?**

* Esperienza di almeno **2 anni** in ruoli di **Business Analyst, PMO o Compliance support**
* Conoscenza della normativa **DORA** e **NIS2**
* Capacità di redigere **procedure operative strutturate** e documentazione formale
* Buona conoscenza delle **infrastrutture ICT** e dei relativi processi/procedure
* Esperienza maturata in **contesti assicurativi**
* Ottime capacità relazionali, organizzative e comunicative
* Capacità di coordinamento tra stakeholder differenti
* Precisione nella gestione documentale e attenzione al dettaglio

**Sei la persona ideale se…**

* Hai esperienza in ambienti regolamentati e compliance\-driven
* Sai muoverti con efficacia tra interlocutori business, IT e funzioni corporate
* Hai approccio metodico e forte orientamento all’organizzazione
* Sei autonomo nella gestione delle attività operative e nel monitoraggio avanzamenti

**Cosa offriamo?**

\- RAL commisurata in base alla seniority

\-BUONI PASTO (anche in modalità full remote)

\-ASSICURAZIONE SANITARIA CON COPERTURA INTEGRATIVA

**I nostri benefit**

PERCORSI DI CARRIERA

Abbiamo un articolato sistema di valutazione delle competenze e misurazione delle performance che consente al dipendente di crescere insieme a noi.

FORMAZIONE PROFESSIONALIZZANTE

Offriamo percorsi di formazione e di aggiornamento professionale con taglio tecnico/pratico su tematiche in ambito ICT.

SCONTI E PROMOZIONI RISERVATE

Abbiamo stabilito degli accordi con delle piattaforme per riservare ai nostri dipendenti scontistica e promozioni esclusive.
